parsed_review.summary: CP00-451 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack covering the RP14.P09 test/Hermes slice (40 units, Risk B, range RP14.P09.M07.S10-RP14.P09.M09.S07; micro-phase rows 13/20/7; deliverables impl:25/claude_review:6/hermes_evidence:1/security_audit:4/test:2/ui:2 summing to 40). All seven required public exports exist (service.js + validators.js) and re-export through the index.js barrel. The descriptor composes upstream CP450 output and is frozen with a complete no-write / no-real-data flag set (freezeCp451Result), and the validators actively enforce descriptor-only rows and the authority boundary: validateSettlementCoreCp451P09TestHermesSliceDescriptor (validators.js:4601-4608) rejects any promotion of Claude to final approval, any enterprise-trust-from-local-validation claim, and any claude_is_final_approval drift. The contract projection (contracts/settlement-core-contract.json p09_test_hermes_slice_descriptor) is deep-equal verified against the code-generated descriptor by both the validator script (1419-1423) and model.test.js, so there is no code/contract drift. Hermes is used solely as a deterministic review packet/receipt descriptor (H14 evidence, receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product false) and not for product runtime. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob; read_only true) and not the final approver; human approval and the enterprise trust claim are handled outside this receipt. The pack honestly declares production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review (command-evidence and manifest agree), exposing only a production_ready_candidate from the Hermes packet, which is the correct deferred posture. CP178+ hardening is satisfied: the inherited forbidden_review_evidence list covers auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims; the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid (non-empty, well-formed, unfenced) JSON and exit 0. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) correctly do not yet exist for CP00-451 because they are produced downstream of this review (steps 4-7 of the hardening sequence); the completed CP00-450 directory contains them. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic note on the generated changed-file-scope artifact. Note: this verdict is independent-reviewer advisory only and does not constitute final approval.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists the pack's own evidence directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-451/ (and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md) appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The cp00-451/ directory is part of CP00-451: it holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json, is referenced in required_pack_artifacts, and the commit_policy commits it under message 'Close CP00-451 settlement core p09 test hermes slice descriptor pack'. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:1719-1727) correctly EXCLUDES this directory, so the discrepancy is confined to the generated scope artifact and reflects a 'preserved-in-worktree' vs 'unrelated-to-pack' naming conflation. Importantly, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required file is dropped from scope and pack integrity is unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the changed-file-scope generator so a pack's own evidence directory is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (or rename the field to reflect 'preserved in worktree'). No code, contract, test, or evidence change is required for CP00-451 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational; does not affect scope integrity, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, the authority boundary, or the deferred production_ready posture. Safe to close out as-is.
